pattern: Because squaring is monotonic on the positive integers, a binary search over candidate roots converges on whether some integer squared equals the input without ever computing a square root.
hints: You only need an integer root, and the square function increases as the candidate grows, so a value's square tells you whether to look higher or lower.
Search the candidate root in the range from one to the number itself, comparing the candidate squared against the target.
Binary search the midpoint m: if m*m equals num return true, if it is smaller move the lower bound up, otherwise move the upper bound down; return false when the range empties.

edge_inputs.description: not a perfect square, its root is between two integers
smallest input, one is one squared
one less than a perfect square
large value just below a perfect square
maximum signed 32-bit value, not a perfect square

stress.description: mix of guaranteed perfect squares and random non-squares across the full range
stress.note: 46340 squared stays within the 32-bit bound; half the draws are exact squares
